topic.what: Unified Compliance Aggregator (UCA) framework integrating six open-source security tools into a single normalized composite security score
topic.how: Normalizes heterogeneous tool outputs to 0-100 scale using domain-specific formulas (linear for compliance, logarithmic for AIDE), applies weighted aggregation with multi-domain tools at 0.20 and single-domain at 0.15
topic.why_matters: Security assessments use multiple independent tools with fragmented outputs; UCA provides unified interpretable metric for overall Linux system security posture

characteristics.what_it_measures: Overall UCA security score change across hardening levels
characteristics.context: Baseline to Full hardening on FABRIC testbed

characteristics.evidence: Table 9; firewall activation contributed 71.7% of total improvement
characteristics.context: Largest single driver; baseline had 2 open ports 4 confirmed vulns, partial/full had firewall filtering 65534 ports
Lynis OpenSCAP CIS STIG all improved with hardening

characteristics.what_it_measures: AIDE score decrease from hardening-induced changes
characteristics.evidence: Table 8: 46 to 317 total changes detected; logarithmic formula prevented collapse
characteristics.context: Logarithmic formula SAIDE = max(0, 100 - 10*log10(Vtotal)) resolved linear collapse limitation

characteristics.vs_baseline: same improvement direction on VMware and FABRIC
characteristics.context: Absolute scores differ (platform configs) but trends consistent confirming platform-independent methodology

characteristics.what_it_measures: Opposing score directions between tool categories
characteristics.vs_baseline: single-tool assessment would miss complete picture
characteristics.context: Compliance tools trend upward file integrity trend downward during hardening; validates multi-tool aggregation value

novelty: first open-source framework integrating six security tools with normalized composite scoring
logarithmic AIDE formula preventing score collapse beyond 20 changes
empirical evidence of divergent tool behavior during hardening
cross-platform FABRIC and VMware validation of methodology
open_problems: static manual weights need ML-based optimization
single experimental iteration lacks statistical depth
cross-distribution (CentOS Debian Rocky) and cloud (AWS Azure GCP) support needed
